observations.label: Deterministic ingestion test fixture document
observations.description: A structured markdown document with nonce XQVR7-ZEPHYR-CANARY-9F3K used as a deterministic test fixture for the generic ingestion orchestrator.
observations.evidence: This document is a deterministic test fixture for the generic ingestion orchestrator. The nonce string XQVR7-ZEPHYR-CANARY-9F3K appears nowhere else in the repository and must be retrievable via query-back.

observations.label: Decomposition phase must never be skipped
observations.description: Agents processing this fixture are prohibited from skipping the decomposition phase of the ingestion pipeline.
observations.evidence: Agents must never skip the decomposition phase when processing this fixture.

observations.label: Minimum primitive type coverage required
observations.description: The pipeline must produce at least one observation for each primitive type present in the content, with state, constraint, and resource as the minimum required set.
observations.evidence: The pipeline must always produce at least one observation of each primitive type that appears in the content. Required coverage includes state, constraint, and resource primitives at minimum.

observations.label: Ingestion orchestrator separates source from pipeline
observations.description: The generic ingestion orchestrator architecture maintains separation between the source (data provider) and the processing pipeline stages.
observations.evidence: The generic ingestion orchestrator separates source from pipeline

observations.label: Six-stage canonical ingestion pipeline execution
observations.description: The pipeline executes a fixed sequence: perceive, interpret, decompose, map, persist, and query, each producing a typed artifact.
observations.evidence: Source.read() → perceive → interpret → decompose → map → persist → query

observations.label: Orchestrator must not modify existing contracts
observations.description: The orchestrator is restricted to sequencing pipeline stages and must never alter the contracts between them.
observations.evidence: The orchestrator never modifies existing contracts — it sequences them.

observations.label: Nonce retrievable at rank 1 after query-back
observations.description: After full pipeline processing, the nonce XQVR7-ZEPHYR-CANARY-9F3K must be retrievable at rank 1 in query results, validating end-to-end persistence.
observations.evidence: Query: nonce XQVR7-ZEPHYR-CANARY-9F3K retrievable at rank 1

observations.label: Fixture immutability requirement
observations.description: The fixture must remain unchanged after creation; if the orchestrator cannot process it, the orchestrator must be adjusted, never the fixture.
observations.evidence: The fixture must remain unchanged after creation. If the orchestrator cannot process it, the orchestrator needs adjustment — never the fixture.

observations.label: Fixture creation date 2026-05-12
observations.description: The test fixture was created on 2026-05-12 specifically for the ingestion-orchestrator-1 validation effort.
observations.evidence: This fixture was created on 2026-05-12 for ingestion-orchestrator-1.

observations.label: Fixture document structure metrics
observations.description: The fixture contains approximately 500 words, 5 headings, 2 constraints, 1 code block, and 1 wikilink as its structural composition.
observations.evidence: Word count | ~500 | Heading count | 5 | Constraints | 2 | Code blocks | 1 | Wikilinks | 1

relationships.description: The six-stage pipeline execution ultimately produces the query-back outcome where the nonce is retrievable at rank 1.
The decomposition-must-not-be-skipped constraint governs how the pipeline execution must proceed.
The no-contract-modification constraint preserves the source-pipeline separation architecture.
The test fixture document serves as the input resource that enables the pipeline execution to run.
The immutability requirement constrains the fixture resource, ensuring it is never modified to accommodate orchestrator deficiencies.
The minimum primitive type coverage requirement establishes measurable criteria that the query-back outcome must satisfy.
